name: Kingdom of Hejaz (1917–1920)

colors.symbolism: the Hashemite dynasty and the Arab Revolt

description: Three horizontal stripes — black, green, white from top to bottom — with a red triangle at the hoist.
history: The flag of the Arab Revolt against the Ottoman Empire (1916), proclaimed under Sharif Hussein bin Ali of Mecca. After the revolt, this design became the flag of the short-lived Kingdom of Hejaz (proclaimed 1916, internationally recognised 1917). The pattern — three stripes plus a coloured triangle — has inspired the flags of most Pan-Arab states (Jordan, Palestine, Iraq, Syria, etc.).
